title: Grow Green

pages.text: Sita was enjoying the beauty of the trees swaying in the heavy rain.
The next-door family came in, saying their house was flooded.
Sita asked her mother, "Why, Mama, is their house flooded and not ours?"
Mother explained, "Because their garden is completely paved with garden tiles, and they have no trees within their compound."
"So, if the drainage blocks, the water has nowhere to go, so it enters the house."
"Within our compound, we have soil and many trees to absorb the water, so we are not flooded."
Sita's next-door friend, Anne, listened to this and said, "Mama, we should plant trees too, and pull out the garden tiles!"
Sita's mother smiled at her and said, "Not only that, darling, you also get pure air when you have trees."
Sita and Anne said, "Yes! We can grow flowering plants too to make our gardens beautiful."
Let's grow green and make our gardens greener!

moral: Plant more trees and protect nature. Trees help prevent floods, give us clean air, and make our world healthier and more beautiful.
